hero.title: Sharp Thinking, Practical Execution
hero.lede: With Martlet, Martijn brings sharp, critical technical thinking and execution. Not just to write reports, but to solve problems for real - together with the team on the shop floor.
hero.sublede: The Netherlands must keep making. That takes companies that keep improving - in product, process and automation.
about.title: About Martijn
about.intro: Martijn is an intellectual pragmatist. He increases decision certainty when choices are expensive — by bringing sharpness and adapting until it works.
about.p1: Martijn is an intellectual pragmatist. He helps organizations first sharpen what the real problem is, before solutions are chosen. From an overview, he brings structure, tests ideas against their purpose and reality, and helps set direction that remains executable.
about.p2: He moves just as easily between problem definition and solution as in strategic conversations and on the shop floor. That continuous switching brings calm, focus and support. People get motivated because they understand why choices are made — and because they can contribute themselves.
about.p3: Martijn worked for eight years in an SME manufacturing company where he arrived as “that Ph.D. who will probably leave again soon.” He stayed, learned the company from the inside, and grew into a reliable point of knowledge who provided direction when it got complex.
about.p4: Driven and deeply involved. If he believes in the direction, he goes for it — exactly what Martlet stands for: keep moving until there is progress.
why.p1: In SME manufacturing companies, decisions often become expensive before they are final: in technology, in systems and in the way of working. That is where uncertainty appears — assumptions stay implicit and execution slowly drifts from intent.
why.p2: Martlet exists to raise decision certainty when it matters. Martijn makes assumptions explicit, tests them for risk and impact, and keeps guiding until direction and execution line up again.
why.callout.title: Decision certainty is a competitive advantage.
why.callout.body: Good choices require sharpness and guidance in execution. Martijn stays involved, adjusts where needed, and keeps practice aligned with direction — or lets direction move when practice demands it.
how.lede: We do not start with assumptions, but with clarity.
how.li1: Is this really a problem? What do we see, what do we measure, what does it cost?
how.li2: Why is this a problem? Cause over symptom. Processes, technology and context.
how.li3: What is the smallest intervention with the biggest effect? Focus over scope.
how.li4: Technology alone is rarely enough. Change often fails on people, ownership and rhythm.
how.li5: Collaboration is required. Without buy-in there is no lasting improvement. Martlet works with the existing team, not alongside it.
how.p2: Martlet combines analysis with delivery. If it works on paper but not on the floor, it is not done.
what.p1: Martlet helps SME manufacturers hold direction in technology and digitalisation — especially when choices become costly. Not with reports and handoff, but by increasing decision certainty and adapting until execution and direction align. Martlet is Martijn's work. In practice you always work directly with him.
what.s1.p: Technical development is about reducing uncertainty. Martijn makes assumptions explicit, tests them for risk and impact, and safeguards direction while the team builds and tests.
what.s1.meta1: For whom: Companies that must make technical choices with major consequences — in new development or in persistent machine problems.
what.s2.p: Overview, direction and execution in digitalisation trajectories. Martijn helps determine what is really needed, tests systems against practice, and adjusts when it grates — until it works on the floor.
what.s2.meta1: For whom: Companies that want to digitalise but lack overview, or that have systems that do not deliver what they promise.
what.s3.p: Temporary reinforcement as project lead, engineering lead or innovation manager. Unblock stuck projects and deliver.
what.s3.meta1: For whom: Companies that temporarily need extra thinking and execution power to move forward.
fit.li1: Choices with major impact must be made, but assumptions remain implicit.
fit.li2: Execution drifts away from the intent — or the intent turns out not to hold.
fit.li3: You want to hold direction in technical development: testing, building and adjusting without chaos.
fit.li4: Digitalisation requires overview, but you want systems that follow practice, not the other way around.
fit.li5: You want guidance that stays: sharpness at the table and presence in execution.
steps.li4: Deliver & embed - implement, train, improve, hand over ownership
values.li1: Ownership: we stay until direction and execution line up
values.li2: Sharpness: assumptions explicit, choices tested for risk and impact
values.li3: Hands-on: present where it happens, together with the team
values.li4: Pragmatic: small steps, big effect — without losing direction
values.li5: Craftsmanship: understanding of technology and manufacturing practice
cta.h: Want to bring sharpness and focus to the table?
cta.p: Schedule a short call. Then we test together: is this really a problem, why, and what is the shortest route to results.
footer: Martlet - Thinking and doing for SME manufacturing.
